title: Foreign Minister Penny Wong speaks on Middle East conflict | 7NEWS
description: The conflict in the Middle East has escalated beyond the region, with the US sinking an Iranian warship off Sri Lanka's coast and NATO intercepting an Iranian ballistic missile targeting Turkey. Iran has now struck 11 countries in the first 72 hours of expanded hostilities, including attacks on travel hubs in the United Arab Emirates. Australian Foreign Minister Penny Wong discusses the unpredictable nature of the spreading conflict, evacuation efforts for Australians in the region, and the importance of defence capabilities including the AUKUS partnership. title: United States sinks Iranian warship off Sri Lanka | 7NEWS
description: A U.S. submarine has struck and sunk an Iranian warship, the Iris Denner, in the Indian Ocean off the coast of Sri Lanka, marking the first enemy vessel destroyed by the U.S. since World War II and the first submarine torpedo sinking in eight decades. At least 87 bodies have been recovered with 32 crew members rescued by Sri Lankan authorities. Meanwhile, Israel continues military operations in Lebanon against Hezbollah targets, displacing 70,000 people, while Operation Epic Fury has hit 2,000 targets and sunk 20 vessels, with U.S. intelligence reporting Iran's ballistic missile stocks reduced by 86%. title: United States reveal Trump assassination plot behind Iran strikes | 7NEWS
description: The United States has launched military strikes on Iran, with Secretary of War Pete Hegseth revealing that a 2024 plot to assassinate Donald Trump was a core motivation. The US claims to have killed the leader of the covert Iranian unit behind the assassination conspiracy, with more than 2,000 Iranian targets already destroyed in Operation Epic Fury. Despite Trump administration claims of broad support, recent polls show most Americans disapprove of the strikes amid concerns about the President's quick resort to military action. title: Stranded Australians return home from Dubai | 7NEWS
description: Around 200 Australians stranded in Dubai due to conflict in Iran arrived safely at Sydney Airport on Emirates flight EK414, reuniting with families who had been waiting to greet them. The Australian government is arranging three additional flights over the next 24 hours to Sydney and Melbourne, with approximately 115,000 Australians still remaining in the Middle East. Emotional scenes of relief marked the arrivals as families were reunited after days of uncertainty in the conflict zone. title: NATO intercepts Iranian missile headed for Turkey | 7NEWS
description: NATO defences intercepted an Iranian ballistic missile over Syria that was heading towards a Turkish military base hosting American forces and approximately 50 nuclear warheads. The incident has raised concerns about the potential activation of NATO's Article 5, which states that an attack on one member nation is considered an attack on all 32 alliance members. The US destroyer in the eastern Mediterranean successfully shot down the missile before it could reach Turkish airspace, while Britain's HMS Dragon deployment to defend RAF Akrotiri in Cyprus has been delayed until next week. title: Canada's PM calls for middle power alliance | 7NEWS
description: Canadian Prime Minister Mark Carney addressed Australia's Federal Parliament, calling for middle powers like Australia and Canada to form new coalitions based on common values and work together on defence, critical minerals and artificial intelligence. The speech comes as the Iran conflict in the Middle East causes economic impacts in Australia, with urea fertiliser prices jumping $150 per tonne and shipping halted in the Strait of Hormuz. Carney warned that without cooperation between 'strategic cousins', hegemons will increasingly dictate global outcomes. title: Greg Lynn denied bail before murder retrial | 7NEWS
description: Greg Lynn, accused of killing high country camper Carol Clay, has been denied bail by the Supreme Court of Appeal in Melbourne ahead of his retrial. Lynn appeared in court briefly, dressed in a black suit, with his wife and son present. The prosecution indicates the murder retrial could begin in six months, while the defence argues it needs years to ensure a fair trial. title: F1 fever hits Melbourne for season opener | 7NEWS
description: The Australian Grand Prix returns to Melbourne as the Formula One season opener for 2026, with dedicated fans queuing overnight and sprinting through the gates to secure prime positions along Melbourne Walk, where F1 drivers sign autographs and pose for photos. title: Queensland proposes age limits for e-bikes and e-scooters | 7NEWS
description: A Queensland parliamentary committee has released 28 recommendations to improve e-bike and e-scooter safety following a year-long inquiry into increasing serious crashes. The key proposals include setting a minimum age of 16 years for riders, requiring at least a learner licence, and allowing Queensland Police to impound illegal devices on first infringement. The recommendations also enable parents to be held accountable for younger children's violations through the State Penalties Enforcement Registry, while 16 and 17 year olds could face direct penalties.
